data.description:
## Context

Placeholder epic for R-1 part 2 from the DSO gap analysis (~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md, R-1). Deferred from the main DSO-adoption epic for its own design session because it touches the Pass-1 chunking machinery + config surface and is a genuine cost-vs-rigor tradeoff.

### The tension
rebar deliberately BIN-PACKS same-facet Pass-1 criteria into one LLM call (a 'chunk') for cost + prompt-cache efficiency, accepting cross-criterion contamination WITHIN a chunk, and relies on the independent Pass-2 verifier + deterministic arithmetic to filter contamination artifacts. DSO takes the opposite stance: it prohibits merging reviewers into one call ('each perspective can be influenced by the others').

### The proposal (converged in design discussion)
A CONFIG FLAG, not a rewrite:
- Default (flag off) = current bin-packing. No behavior change; back-out is flipping it off.
- Flag on = every BLOCKING-ELIGIBLE criterion executes in its OWN isolated context window (co-residency = 1), so the highest-stakes findings (the only ones that can block a claim) are never contaminated by chunk neighbors. The advisory tail stays bin-packed (bounded cost).
- Rationale: spend isolation cost ONLY where a wrong finding changes an outcome (blocks a claim) -- same 'spend rigor where it matters' logic as the 0.70 blocking threshold and R-2 targeted redundancy.

### Evidence gate (why this is deferred, not built now)
rebar's STATED position is that the Pass-2 verifier already catches contamination. This proposal bets that verifier-catching is imperfect for blocking findings -- a bet that must be TESTED before building. It depends on the R-1 part-1 'cohort' observability field (shipping in the main DSO-adoption epic): each Pass-1 finding gets stamped with its chunk cohort (sorted criterion-id set) in pass1_chunk (passes.py:294-306), carried through _slim (sidecar.py:236-264). Only with that field can an offline analysis measure 'do blocking-tier findings actually get contaminated by their co-resident criteria?' Flip this flag on (like novelty_drop_active / completion_floor_active) only after cohort analysis clears the bar.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Cohort analysis over accumulated sidecar data quantifies blocking-tier contamination rate BEFORE any isolation is enabled
- [ ] If justified: a config flag (default off) isolating blocking-eligible criteria into their own context windows, integrated with the chunking/token-budget machinery
- [ ] Advisory-tier criteria remain bin-packed regardless of the flag
- [ ] Cost delta of the isolated path measured + documented

## Context (dependencies)
- Depends on the cohort observability field (R-1 part 1) landing first in the main DSO-adoption epic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] This epic holds the full design rationale + the cost-vs-rigor discussion
- [ ] Not to be implemented until (a) the cohort field ships and (b) sidecar evidence shows blocking-tier contamination is real
- [ ] Placeholder only pending a dedicated design/eval session
data.title: Plan-review: config-flagged per-criterion context isolation for blocking-tier criteria (R-1 part 2)
